problem: Expand helix-cli from a narrow inspection tool (inspect + comments) into an org-aware Helix workbench supporting org switching, ticket discovery/filtering, ticket detail inspection (branch/repos/runs/artifacts), local Codex bundling, ticket creation, comment reply, rerun, and continue.

files.why: CLI entry point and top-level command router. Currently handles: login, inspect, comments, --version. All new command groups (org, tickets) must be registered here.
Auth config storage (~/.hlx/config.json). Currently stores {apiKey, url}. Org switching (switch-org returns new JWT) requires persisting the new accessToken. May need to store current org metadata.
HTTP client (hxFetch) with retry, auth headers, basePath. Default basePath is '/api/inspect'. New ticket/org commands need basePath '/api'. Already supports GET/POST, queryParams, body.
Repository resolution utility. Uses '/api/inspect/repositories' endpoint. May serve as pattern reference for new org/ticket resolution.
OAuth + manual login flow. Saves config via saveConfig(). The OAuth callback returns a key (API key), while org switching returns a session JWT (accessToken) - different auth header behavior.
Existing comment command router. Uses getFlag() helper, resolveTicketId() for --ticket/HELIX_TICKET_ID. Pattern reference for new command groups.
Existing comment list command. Uses hxFetch with basePath '/api'. Shows pattern for fetching and displaying data.
Existing comment post command. Uses hxFetch POST with basePath '/api'. Pattern for write operations.
Inspect subcommand router. Uses getFlag() and getPositionalArgs() helpers. Pattern reference for new subcommand routing.
Project manifest. Version 1.2.0, zero production deps, bin entry 'hlx'. Scripts: build (tsc), typecheck (tsc --noEmit). ESM module type.
TypeScript config. Target ES2022, module Node16, strict, declaration. Imports use .js extensions for ESM.

facts: CLI entry point is src/index.ts with shebang, uses switch-based routing for top-level commands (login, inspect, comments)
Package is @projectxinnovation/helix-cli v1.2.0, zero production dependencies, TypeScript only dev deps
Auth config stored at ~/.hlx/config.json as {apiKey: string, url: string}. Env vars HELIX_API_KEY/HELIX_URL take priority
Tokens starting with 'hxi_' use X-API-Key header; others use Authorization: Bearer header (src/lib/http.ts:53-57)
hxFetch default basePath is '/api/inspect'. Comments already use basePath '/api'. New commands will need basePath '/api'
Manual flag parsing throughout (indexOf-based getFlag helper). No CLI framework (commander, yargs, etc.)
No tests exist. No testing framework configured. No test scripts in package.json
No README exists in the repository
Current command surface: hlx login, hlx inspect (repos|db|logs|api), hlx comments (list|post), hlx --version
ESM module with .js extension imports required for Node16 module resolution
hxFetch supports retry (3 attempts, exponential backoff), 30s timeout, 429 Retry-After handling
HELIX_TICKET_ID env var resolves ticket context for comments commands (src/comments/index.ts:12-18)
Version is hardcoded as '0.1.0' in src/index.ts:47 (mismatches package.json 1.2.0)
unknowns: How should org switch persist the new JWT token? switch-org returns accessToken (session JWT), but current config stores apiKey which may be an hxi_ key. The CLI may need to store both or distinguish between session tokens and API keys.
Should 'hlx tickets latest' be a dedicated backend endpoint or client-side (first item from sorted list)?
What is the expected local bundle structure for 'hlx tickets bundle'? The ticket says 'deterministic local context folder' but the exact file layout is not specified.
How should the CLI resolve user IDs for the --user filter? By email, name, or ID? The backend org members endpoint (GET /api/organization/members) returns {id, email, name}.
Should the CLI support pagination for ticket listing, or is the current backend behavior (return all matching tickets) sufficient?
